title: Mortgage Rate Compression Refinance Wave Fade
thesis: When MORTGAGE30US drops >0.5% over 2 weeks and refinance search interest spikes, consumer discretionary spending rebounds but eventual rate stabilization causes mean reversion. Falling rates unlock household liquidity and home equity; consumers spend on discretionary goods and services.
data_sources: FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) daily; pair with Google Trends weekly searches for 'mortgage refinance' and 'home equity loan'
signal_logic: If MORTGAGE30US drops >0.5% in 2 weeks AND Google Trends 'refinance' searches rise >40% YoY, buy XLY
entry_exit: Entry: If MORTGAGE30US drops >0.5% in 2 weeks AND Google Trends 'refinance' searches rise >40% YoY, buy XLY Exit: After 12 trading days or when rates stabilize for 3+ consecutive days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) daily; pair with Google Trends weekly searches for 'mortgage refinance' and 'home equity loan' via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Fed policy shifts and bond market repricing trigger rate drops several times per year, reliably firing this signal.

schema.idea: Mortgage Rate Compression Refinance Wave Fade

data.source: FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) daily; pair with Google Trends weekly searches for 'mortgage refinance' and 'home equity loan'

schema.behavior: When MORTGAGE30US drops >0.5% over 2 weeks and refinance search interest spikes, consumer discretionary spending rebounds but eventual rate stabilization causes mean reversion.

market_impact.rationale: Falling rates unlock household liquidity and home equity; consumers spend on discretionary goods and services.

trade_logic.entry: If MORTGAGE30US drops >0.5% in 2 weeks AND Google Trends 'refinance' searches rise >40% YoY, buy XLY
trade_logic.exit: After 12 trading days or when rates stabilize for 3+ consecutive days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Fed policy shifts and bond market repricing trigger rate drops several times per year, reliably firing this signal.

title: Ev Charger Network Expansion Sentiment Surge
thesis: Spikes in charger installation and concurrent surge in consumer searches signal bullish infrastructure narrative, lifting XLK (chip/software) and XLI (automotive) positioning. EV charging expansion is a positive signal for semiconductor and software demand in automotive electrification.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API weekly count of newly added chargers across US; pair with Google Trends weekly search volume for 'EV charging stations near me'
signal_logic: If weekly new charger count rises >20% QoQ and Google Trends 'EV charging' searches rise >30% YoY
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly new charger count rises >20% QoQ and Google Trends 'EV charging' searches rise >30% YoY Exit: After 4 weeks or if charger growth rate decelerates below 10% QoQ
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API weekly count of newly added chargers across US; pair with Google Trends weekly search volume for 'EV charging stations near me' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Government subsidy cycles and corporate announcements cluster charger expansion; signal fires 3–4 times per year.

schema.idea: EV Charger Network Expansion Sentiment Surge

data.source: OpenChargeMap API weekly count of newly added chargers across US; pair with Google Trends weekly search volume for 'EV charging stations near me'

schema.behavior: Spikes in charger installation and concurrent surge in consumer searches signal bullish infrastructure narrative, lifting XLK (chip/software) and XLI (automotive) positioning.

market_impact.rationale: EV charging expansion is a positive signal for semiconductor and software demand in automotive electrification.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly new charger count rises >20% QoQ and Google Trends 'EV charging' searches rise >30% YoY
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if charger growth rate decelerates below 10% QoQ

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Government subsidy cycles and corporate announcements cluster charger expansion; signal fires 3–4 times per year.

title: Copper Contango Collapse Industrial Demand Proxy
thesis: When copper spot rallies but forward curve flattens (contango narrows), market is signaling near-term demand strength but supply confidence; bullish for materials. Copper demand elasticity is high for construction, manufacturing, and power; curve shape is a sensitive real-economy gauge.
data_sources: FRED series MMNRNJ (Copper prices, spot) daily for spot; pair with CME futures curve slope via price_only adapter (HG nearest vs 6-month forward prices)
signal_logic: If copper spot rises >2% in 3 days AND 6-month forward premium (contango) shrinks to <1.5% annualized
entry_exit: Entry: If copper spot rises >2% in 3 days AND 6-month forward premium (contango) shrinks to <1.5% annualized Exit: After 7 trading days or if contango widens beyond 2.5%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series MMNRNJ (Copper prices, spot) daily for spot; pair with CME futures curve slope via price_only adapter (HG nearest vs 6-month forward prices) via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity curve dynamics shift weekly with supply/demand news; this signal fires 2–3 times per month.

schema.idea: Copper Contango Collapse Industrial Demand Proxy

data.source: FRED series MMNRNJ (Copper prices, spot) daily for spot; pair with CME futures curve slope via price_only adapter (HG nearest vs 6-month forward prices)

schema.behavior: When copper spot rallies but forward curve flattens (contango narrows), market is signaling near-term demand strength but supply confidence; bullish for materials.

market_impact.rationale: Copper demand elasticity is high for construction, manufacturing, and power; curve shape is a sensitive real-economy gauge.

trade_logic.entry: If copper spot rises >2% in 3 days AND 6-month forward premium (contango) shrinks to <1.5% annualized
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if contango widens beyond 2.5%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity curve dynamics shift weekly with supply/demand news; this signal fires 2–3 times per month.

title: Healthcare Procedure Google Trends Demand Resurgence
thesis: Spikes in elective procedure searches signal consumer confidence and disposable income recovery; bullish for consumer discretionary and healthcare services. Elective healthcare spending is a proxy for consumer financial health and willingness to spend on non-essentials.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'cosmetic surgery near me', 'dental implant cost', 'laser eye surgery'
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends for elective procedure searches rises >50% YoY and XLY closes up >0.5% that week
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends for elective procedure searches rises >50% YoY and XLY closes up >0.5% that week Exit: After 3 weeks or if search interest falls below baseline for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'cosmetic surgery near me', 'dental implant cost', 'laser eye surgery' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal consumer confidence waves (post-bonus, tax refunds, holiday spending) reliably drive elective procedure searches.

schema.idea: Healthcare Procedure Google Trends Demand Resurgence

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'cosmetic surgery near me', 'dental implant cost', 'laser eye surgery'

schema.behavior: Spikes in elective procedure searches signal consumer confidence and disposable income recovery; bullish for consumer discretionary and healthcare services.

market_impact.rationale: Elective healthcare spending is a proxy for consumer financial health and willingness to spend on non-essentials.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends for elective procedure searches rises >50% YoY and XLY closes up >0.5% that week
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or if search interest falls below baseline for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal consumer confidence waves (post-bonus, tax refunds, holiday spending) reliably drive elective procedure searches.

title: Utility Sector Outflow On Mild Weather Pivot
thesis: When rolling 7-day average HDD drops sharply from seasonal baseline, heating demand collapses, reducing utility stock appeal and triggering rotation out of XLU into risk assets. Lower heating demand directly reduces utility revenue and crushes relative valuation of defensive utility stocks.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily heating degree days (HDD) and cooling degree days (CDD) for US population-weighted centers
signal_logic: If 7-day rolling HDD falls below historical seasonal average by >15% and XLU closes down 1%+ that day
entry_exit: Entry: If 7-day rolling HDD falls below historical seasonal average by >15% and XLU closes down 1%+ that day Exit: After 10 trading days or when HDD returns to seasonal norm
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily heating degree days (HDD) and cooling degree days (CDD) for US population-weighted centers via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather patterns shift weekly; mild snaps occur multiple times per winter/spring season, reliably triggering HDD collapses.

schema.idea: Utility Sector Outflow on Mild Weather Pivot

data.source: Open-Meteo daily heating degree days (HDD) and cooling degree days (CDD) for US population-weighted centers

schema.behavior: When rolling 7-day average HDD drops sharply from seasonal baseline, heating demand collapses, reducing utility stock appeal and triggering rotation out of XLU into risk assets.

market_impact.rationale: Lower heating demand directly reduces utility revenue and crushes relative valuation of defensive utility stocks.

trade_logic.entry: If 7-day rolling HDD falls below historical seasonal average by >15% and XLU closes down 1%+ that day
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when HDD returns to seasonal norm

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather patterns shift weekly; mild snaps occur multiple times per winter/spring season, reliably triggering HDD collapses.

title: Agricultural Commodity Volatility Inversion Farmland Stress
thesis: When corn and wheat volatility invert (one spikes while the other collapses), it signals supply/demand dislocations that stress rural credit and farm equipment demand. Agricultural equipment manufacturers (John Deere, AGCO) are sensitive to farm profitability; commodity volatility inversions signal margin compression.
data_sources: FRED series PPIACO (Corn price) and PPIWHE (Wheat price) daily; calculate 10-day rolling volatility for each
signal_logic: If 10-day corn vol rises >50% while wheat vol falls >30%, and XLI closes down >1%
entry_exit: Entry: If 10-day corn vol rises >50% while wheat vol falls >30%, and XLI closes down >1% Exit: After 6 trading days or when both volatilities normalize within 20% of each other
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series PPIACO (Corn price) and PPIWHE (Wheat price) daily; calculate 10-day rolling volatility for each via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal weather, USDA reports, and geopolitical events drive commodity vol spikes monthly.

schema.idea: Agricultural Commodity Volatility Inversion Farmland Stress

data.source: FRED series PPIACO (Corn price) and PPIWHE (Wheat price) daily; calculate 10-day rolling volatility for each

schema.behavior: When corn and wheat volatility invert (one spikes while the other collapses), it signals supply/demand dislocations that stress rural credit and farm equipment demand.

market_impact.rationale: Agricultural equipment manufacturers (John Deere, AGCO) are sensitive to farm profitability; commodity volatility inversions signal margin compression.

trade_logic.entry: If 10-day corn vol rises >50% while wheat vol falls >30%, and XLI closes down >1%
trade_logic.exit: After 6 trading days or when both volatilities normalize within 20% of each other

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal weather, USDA reports, and geopolitical events drive commodity vol spikes monthly.

title: Freight Rail Utilization Recovery Signal
thesis: Weekly carload volume spikes above 12-week moving average signal sudden demand recovery in manufacturing and commodities, bullish for industrials and materials. Rail volume is a leading proxy for real economic activity; spikes correlate with factory orders and industrial production acceleration.
data_sources: AAR (Association of American Railroads) weekly carload data via FRED series RAILCDF (carloads and intermodal) or scrape public AAR reports
signal_logic: If weekly carload count exceeds 12-week MA by >5% and volume increases YoY
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly carload count exceeds 12-week MA by >5% and volume increases YoY Exit: After 3 weeks or when carloads fall back below 12-week MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use AAR (Association of American Railroads) weekly carload data via FRED series RAILCDF (carloads and intermodal) or scrape public AAR reports via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal demand swings and supply-chain normalization events trigger rail spikes several times per quarter.

data.source: AAR (Association of American Railroads) weekly carload data via FRED series RAILCDF (carloads and intermodal) or scrape public AAR reports

schema.behavior: Weekly carload volume spikes above 12-week moving average signal sudden demand recovery in manufacturing and commodities, bullish for industrials and materials.

market_impact.rationale: Rail volume is a leading proxy for real economic activity; spikes correlate with factory orders and industrial production acceleration.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly carload count exceeds 12-week MA by >5% and volume increases YoY
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or when carloads fall back below 12-week MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal demand swings and supply-chain normalization events trigger rail spikes several times per quarter.

title: Natural Gas Volatility Spike Reversal In Energy
thesis: When 20-day rolling volatility of natural gas prices spikes above 2-year 95th percentile, mean reversion in energy equities often follows as supply shocks resolve. Extreme gas price volatility signals supply disruption; reversal attracts energy sector value buyers and reduces hedge costs.
data_sources: FRED series DHHNGSP (Henry Hub natural gas spot price) daily closes for volatility calc
signal_logic: If 20-day rolling vol of DHHNGSP > 95th percentile over 2 years and XLE closes up >1% following a down day
entry_exit: Entry: If 20-day rolling vol of DHHNGSP > 95th percentile over 2 years and XLE closes up >1% following a down day Exit: After 8 trading days or when 20-day vol falls below 80th percentile
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series DHHNGSP (Henry Hub natural gas spot price) daily closes for volatility calc via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal geopolitical and weather shocks drive gas price spikes multiple times per quarter.

schema.idea: Natural Gas Volatility Spike Reversal in Energy

data.source: FRED series DHHNGSP (Henry Hub natural gas spot price) daily closes for volatility calc

schema.behavior: When 20-day rolling volatility of natural gas prices spikes above 2-year 95th percentile, mean reversion in energy equities often follows as supply shocks resolve.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme gas price volatility signals supply disruption; reversal attracts energy sector value buyers and reduces hedge costs.

trade_logic.entry: If 20-day rolling vol of DHHNGSP > 95th percentile over 2 years and XLE closes up >1% following a down day
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or when 20-day vol falls below 80th percentile

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal geopolitical and weather shocks drive gas price spikes multiple times per quarter.

title: Bankruptcy Filings Surge Corporate Stress Wave
thesis: Spikes in corporate bankruptcy filings (Chapter 11) signal debt stress across the economy; lag 1–2 weeks before credit spreads widen and equities selloff. Rising corporate bankruptcies increase bank loan losses and reduce confidence in credit quality; financial sector reprices risk higher.
data_sources: US Courts public bankruptcy filing data (Chapter 7, 11, 13) via html_table scrape; weekly aggregation of new filings
signal_logic: If weekly Chapter 11 filings exceed 12-week MA by >20% and XLF closes down >0.5%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Chapter 11 filings exceed 12-week MA by >20% and XLF closes down >0.5% Exit: After 10 trading days or when weekly filings fall below 12-week MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use US Courts public bankruptcy filing data (Chapter 7, 11, 13) via html_table scrape; weekly aggregation of new filings via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Economic cycles and credit tightening events drive bankruptcy waves; signal fires 2–3 times per year.

schema.idea: Bankruptcy Filings Surge Corporate Stress Wave

data.source: US Courts public bankruptcy filing data (Chapter 7, 11, 13) via html_table scrape; weekly aggregation of new filings

schema.behavior: Spikes in corporate bankruptcy filings (Chapter 11) signal debt stress across the economy; lag 1–2 weeks before credit spreads widen and equities selloff.

market_impact.rationale: Rising corporate bankruptcies increase bank loan losses and reduce confidence in credit quality; financial sector reprices risk higher.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Chapter 11 filings exceed 12-week MA by >20% and XLF closes down >0.5%
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when weekly filings fall below 12-week MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Economic cycles and credit tightening events drive bankruptcy waves; signal fires 2–3 times per year.

title: Airline Load Factor Contraction Fare Collapse Signal
thesis: When airline load factors drop >5% week-over-week, it signals weakening travel demand and airlines will cut fares aggressively, pressuring XLY and supporting XLP. Falling load factors and fare wars erode airline and leisure travel margins; consumer is trading down or reducing discretionary trips.
data_sources: BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) weekly airline load factor data via bts_airline_load_factor adapter
signal_logic: If weekly load factor falls >5% WoW and is below 12-week MA
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly load factor falls >5% WoW and is below 12-week MA Exit: After 4 weeks or when load factor recovers above 12-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) weekly airline load factor data via bts_airline_load_factor adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel patterns and economic news cause load factor swings monthly; signal fires 2–4 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Airline Load Factor Contraction Fare Collapse Signal

data.source: BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) weekly airline load factor data via bts_airline_load_factor adapter

schema.behavior: When airline load factors drop >5% week-over-week, it signals weakening travel demand and airlines will cut fares aggressively, pressuring XLY and supporting XLP.

market_impact.rationale: Falling load factors and fare wars erode airline and leisure travel margins; consumer is trading down or reducing discretionary trips.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly load factor falls >5% WoW and is below 12-week MA
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or when load factor recovers above 12-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel patterns and economic news cause load factor swings monthly; signal fires 2–4 times per quarter.
